query: US Navy 7th Fleet Taiwan Strait transit May 15-31 2026
answer:
## Research Report: US Navy 7th Fleet Taiwan Strait Transits — May 15–31, 2026

### Key Finding: No Confirmed US Navy Taiwan Strait Transit During May 15–31, 2026

Based on a thorough review of USNI News (the most authoritative open-source tracker of US naval movements), including their Fleet Tracker (latest: May 18, 2026) and Western Pacific Pulse (latest: May 15, 2026), **no US Navy Taiwan Strait transit has been publicly reported during the May 15–31, 2026 window**.

---

### Most Recent US Navy Taiwan Strait Transit

**January 16–17, 2026** — USS John Finn (DDG-113) & USNS Mary Sears (T-AGS-65)
- This was the **first and, so far, only confirmed US transit of 2026**
- 7th Fleet confirmed the ships conducted a "routine Taiwan Strait transit… through waters where high-seas freedoms of navigation and overflight apply in accordance with international law."
- **China's response**: PLA Eastern Theater Command tracked the ships; China launched **25 sorties** (fighters, support aircraft, UAVs), with six flights crossing the median line.

### Other Notable 2026 Transit (Non-US)

**April 17, 2026** — JMSDF destroyer JS Ikazuchi (DD-107)
- First transit under Japan's Takaichi administration; the **fourth known Japanese transit** in recent years.
- China denounced it as "sending the wrong signals to Taiwan independence separatist forces."

---

### Current Western Pacific Posture (as of May 15–18, 2026)

| Asset | Location/Status |
|-------|-----------------|
| USS George Washington (CVN-73) | Returned to Yokosuka, Japan on May 18 after carrier landing practice near Iwo Jima |
| USS Boxer (LHD-4) ARG + 11th MEU | Operating in 7th Fleet area |
| HMS Spey (P234) | Port visit at Kure Naval Base, Japan (arrived May 14) |
| Balikatan 2026 | Recently concluded US-Philippine exercises |
| Total US Battle Force | 291 ships (232 USS, 59 USNS); 101 deployed; 85 underway |

---

### Transit Frequency Trend: 2025–2026

Under the second Trump administration, Taiwan Strait transits appear to be occurring at a **reduced pace** compared to the Biden era (~monthly):

- **2025**: ~3 US Navy transits (Feb, Apr, Sep), plus several allied transits (Canada ×2, UK, Australia, Japan)
- **2026 (through May 21)**: Only **1 confirmed US transit** (January), plus 1 Japanese transit (April)

This represents a notable **~4-month gap** since the last US transit (January 16–17). However, it is possible transits occurred without public announcement — the January transit was first reported by Chinese officials before US confirmation.

### Complete Timeline of Known Transits, 2025–2026

| Date | Ship(s) | Nation | Key Detail |
|------|---------|--------|------------|
| Feb 10–12, 2025 | USS Ralph Johnson, USNS Bowditch | US | First under Trump 2nd admin |
| Feb 2025 | HMCS Ottawa | Canada | First allied transit of 2025 |
| Apr 23, 2025 | USS William P. Lawrence | US | PLA condemned |
| Jun 12, 2025 | JS Takanami | Japan | En route to Philippine exercise |
| Jun 18, 2025 | HMS Spey | UK | First UK transit since 2021 |
| Sep 6, 2025 | HMAS Brisbane, HMCS Ville de Quebec | AUS/CAN | Joint transit |
| Sep 12, 2025 | USS Higgins, HMS Richmond | US/UK | Joint transit |
| Jan 16–17, 2026 | USS John Finn, USNS Mary Sears | US | 25 Chinese sorties in response |
| Apr 17, 2026 | JS Ikazuchi | Japan | China denounced |

---

### Sources
- USNI News Fleet Tracker (May 18, 2026)
- USNI News Western Pacific Pulse (May 15, 2026)
- USNI News articles and RSS feed (news.usni.org), accessed May 21, 2026

key_facts: No US Navy Taiwan Strait transit has been publicly reported during May 15-31, 2026, based on USNI News Fleet Tracker (May 18) and Western Pacific Pulse (May 15).
The most recent confirmed US Navy Taiwan Strait transit was January 16-17, 2026, by USS John Finn (DDG-113) and USNS Mary Sears (T-AGS-65) — a gap of approximately 4 months.
China responded to the January 2026 transit with 25 sorties (fighters, support aircraft, UAVs), with six flights crossing the Taiwan Strait median line.
Japan's JMSDF destroyer JS Ikazuchi (DD-107) transited the Taiwan Strait on April 17, 2026 — the most recent known transit by any nation.
US Taiwan Strait transit frequency under the second Trump administration appears reduced compared to the Biden era (~monthly): only ~3 US transits in 2025 and 1 confirmed in 2026 through May 21.
Current 7th Fleet posture (May 18, 2026): USS George Washington (CVN-73) returned to Yokosuka; USS Boxer ARG + 11th MEU operating in 7th Fleet area.
Total US Battle Force as of mid-May 2026: 291 ships (232 USS, 59 USNS), 101 deployed, 85 underway.
It is possible US transits have occurred without public announcement; the January 2026 transit was first reported by Chinese officials before US confirmation.
